title: Heptapleurum chartaceum
taxonomy: Heptapleurum chartaceum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. It belongs to the genus Heptapleurum, which is closely related to other genera in the same family. Heptapleurum chartaceum is an evergreen climber with heart-shaped leaves and small white flowers. The classification of Heptapleurum chartaceum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Araliaceae family.

intro: Heptapleurum chartaceum, also known as the "Mokara", is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, belonging to the Araliaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential to alleviate fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, making it a traditional remedy in various Asian cultures. The bioactive constituents of Heptapleurum chartaceum include lignans, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including fever, arthritis, and gastrointestinal problems.
